title: The Practice, Again
scripture_text: Looking to Jesus, the founder and perfecter of our faith.
reflection: You do not have to love your boss to learn from this season. Some lessons are not about the boss. (The third hearing of this day.)
body:
The Sunday evening dread. The Sunday evening weight. The voice in your head running the conversation you are afraid you will have on Monday.

This is hard. Some seasons are just hard. You do not have to pretend they are not.

The Lord is not asking you to enjoy this. He is asking you to stay soft in the middle of it. To not become the thing that is being done to you. To keep your conscience, your kindness, your sleep.

If it is wrong, change what you can. If you cannot change it, find the people who see you, and let them hold the parts of you this job is threatening to take.

This too is a season. Seasons end.

The mind is a wild animal. It has not been tamed. It wanders. It loops. It invents conversations that will never happen. It rehearses grievances from 2007. It makes shopping lists. It composes apologies to people who have died.

The mind, left alone, is exhausting. And you, today, are not alone with it. The Lord of the universe, who holds every thought in the palm of His hand, is in the room with the mind. He is not surprised by it. He made it. He can hold it.

You do not have to master the mind. You only have to bring it back. To the breath. To the word. To the chair. To the now. The Lord meets you in the bringing-back, every time.

One thing, at a time. The next small thing. The Lord meets you in the small, focused moments.

The mind is a wild animal. It has not been tamed. It wanders. It loops. It invents conversations that will never happen. It rehearses grievances from 2007. It makes shopping lists. It composes apologies to people who have died.

The mind, left alone, is exhausting. And you, today, are not alone with it. The Lord of the universe, who holds every thought in the palm of His hand, is in the room with the mind. He is not surprised by it. He made it. He can hold it.

You do not have to master the mind. You only have to bring it back. To the breath. To the word. To the chair. To the now. The Lord meets you in the bringing-back, every time.

There is a way of being in the world that does not require you to be ahead, or impressive, or on top of it. The world will not validate this way. The world will tell you to hurry. The Lord is telling you to be still. The Lord is telling you to be here. The Lord is the one who sees you, right now, in this chair, with this breath, with this much strength, with this much doubt. He sees you. He is with you. The seeing is the practice. The being-with is the gift.

One thing, at a time. The next small thing. The Lord meets you in the small, focused moments.

This is the third time the year has come around to this day. You have been here before. The Lord has been here before. The body remembers. The soul remembers. The grace is the same.
prayer: Lord, be my strength at work. Help me not to become hard. And show me the door when it is time to walk through it. Amen.
